title: Wet AMD with Subfoveal Choroidal Neovascularization
description: A 62-year-old woman presents with sudden painless decrease in central vision in the right eye over two weeks. OCT reveals subfoveal fluid with pigment epithelial detachment, and FFA shows a classic choroidal neovascular membrane. The learner must evaluate treatment options including anti-VEGF therapy.

title: Proliferative Diabetic Retinopathy with Vitreous Hemorrhage
description: A 55-year-old male with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes for 18 years presents with sudden floaters and blurred vision in the left eye. Examination reveals neovascularization at the disc and elsewhere with preretinal hemorrhage. The case requires analysis of staging, PRP versus vitrectomy decision-making, and systemic risk factor management.

title: Primary Open-Angle Glaucoma with Progressive Field Loss
description: A 58-year-old man is referred after routine screening reveals bilateral asymmetric cupping with cup-to-disc ratio of 0.7 in the right eye and 0.5 in the left. IOP is 26 mmHg OD and 22 mmHg OS. Visual field testing shows an early arcuate scotoma in the right eye. The learner must apply principles of glaucoma workup and initiate appropriate medical therapy.

title: Acute Anterior Uveitis in HLA-B27 Positive Patient
description: A 28-year-old woman presents with acute onset of redness, pain, and photophobia in the left eye with a history of recurrent low back pain. Slit-lamp examination reveals fibrinous anterior chamber reaction with posterior synechiae. The learner must analyze the systemic associations, order appropriate investigations, and plan a treatment protocol including topical and systemic therapy.

title: Rhegmatogenous Retinal Detachment with Macula-Off
description: A 45-year-old myopic male presents with a one-week history of flashes, floaters, and a curtain-like shadow in the right eye. B-scan and indirect ophthalmoscopy reveal a superotemporal bullous retinal detachment with a horseshoe tear extending to involve the macula. The learner must evaluate surgical options including scleral buckling versus pars plana vitrectomy and assess prognostic factors for visual recovery.

title: Pediatric Accommodative Esotropia with Amblyopia
description: A 6-year-old boy is brought by his parents with complaints of inward turning of the right eye noticed over the past year. Cover test reveals a 25-prism-diopter esotropia that partially corrects with hyperopic spectacle correction. The right eye shows reduced visual acuity consistent with amblyopia. The learner must apply principles of strabismus evaluation, prescribe appropriate optical correction, and initiate amblyopia therapy.

title: Central Retinal Vein Occlusion with Macular Edema
description: A 67-year-old hypertensive woman presents with painless blurring of vision in the left eye for three days. Fundoscopy reveals dilated tortuous veins with flame-shaped hemorrhages in all four quadrants with disc edema. OCT demonstrates significant cystoid macular edema. The learner must analyze the ischemic versus non-ischemic classification, systemic workup, and plan intravitreal therapy.

title: Progressive Keratoconus with Contact Lens Intolerance
description: A 22-year-old male with a history of allergic eye disease and habitual eye rubbing presents with progressive blurring of vision. Topography reveals inferior steepening with a Kmax of 52 diopters in the right eye. He is intolerant to rigid gas permeable contact lenses. The learner must apply the principles of keratoconus staging and evaluate options including corneal collagen cross-linking.

title: Acute Post-Cataract Surgery Endophthalmitis
description: A 70-year-old woman presents on postoperative day 4 after uneventful phacoemulsification with severe pain, lid edema, and reduced vision to hand movements. Slit-lamp examination reveals a 1.5 mm hypopyon with dense vitritis. The learner must evaluate the case using EVS guidelines, decide on immediate intravitreal antibiotic injection versus tap-and-inject versus vitrectomy, and manage the clinical emergency.

title: Tubercular Posterior Uveitis with Serpiginous-like Choroiditis
description: A 35-year-old male presents with gradual blurring of vision in both eyes. Fundoscopy reveals multifocal serpiginous-like choroidal lesions with vitritis. Mantoux test is strongly positive and chest X-ray shows hilar lymphadenopathy. The learner must analyze the clinical and investigative findings to differentiate tubercular from idiopathic serpiginous choroidopathy and plan ATT along with corticosteroid therapy.

title: Vitamin A Deficiency with Bitot's Spots and Keratomalacia
description: A 4-year-old malnourished girl from a rural tribal area is brought with night blindness and dry, lusterless eyes. Examination reveals bilateral Bitot's spots on the temporal conjunctiva with early corneal xerosis. The learner must understand the WHO classification of xerophthalmia, recognize the clinical stages, and outline the vitamin A supplementation protocol.

title: Thyroid Eye Disease with Compressive Optic Neuropathy
description: A 42-year-old woman with known Graves' disease presents with progressive proptosis, diplopia, and recent color vision loss in the right eye. CT orbit shows enlarged extraocular muscles with crowding at the orbital apex. CAS score is 5/7. The learner must analyze the clinical activity score, differentiate active from inactive disease, and determine the need for urgent orbital decompression versus immunosuppression.

title: Orbital Cellulitis Secondary to Ethmoid Sinusitis
description: A 50-year-old diabetic male presents with rapidly progressive painful proptosis, restricted extraocular movements, and decreased vision in the left eye following an upper respiratory infection. CT scan reveals a subperiosteal abscess along the medial wall with ethmoid sinusitis. The learner must evaluate the Chandler classification, differentiate preseptal from orbital cellulitis, assess for surgical drainage indications, and coordinate ENT consultation.

title: Retinopathy of Prematurity — Screening and Management
description: A preterm infant born at 28 weeks gestational age with birth weight of 1100 grams is referred for ROP screening at 4 weeks of life. RetCam imaging reveals Stage 3 ROP with plus disease in Zone II of both eyes. The learner must create a comprehensive management plan integrating ICROP3 classification, ETROP treatment criteria, and choose between laser photocoagulation and intravitreal anti-VEGF therapy while designing a follow-up protocol.

title: Optic Neuritis with Suspected Demyelinating Disease
description: A 30-year-old woman presents with acute painful loss of vision in the right eye aggravated by eye movements. Examination reveals a relative afferent pupillary defect, dyschromatopsia, and a swollen optic disc. MRI brain shows periventricular white matter lesions. The learner must evaluate the findings in light of the Optic Neuritis Treatment Trial, assess risk of conversion to multiple sclerosis, and determine the role of IV methylprednisolone therapy.

title: Acute Retinal Necrosis — The Race Against Vision Loss
description: A 48-year-old man presents with sudden painful vision loss, periorbital pain, and floaters. Examination reveals anterior uveitis with posterior synechiae and peripheral retinal necrosis. Understanding VZV tropism for arterial endothelium is key.

title: Toxoplasma — When the Old Scar Wakes Up
description: A 25-year-old woman presents with blurred vision and floaters. Fundus shows a focal area of necrotizing retinitis adjacent to an old pigmented scar with overlying vitritis — the classic 'headlight in the fog' appearance.

title: CMV Retinitis — The Brushfire That Never Stops
description: A 38-year-old HIV-positive man on irregular ART presents with painless progressive vision loss. Fundus reveals full-thickness retinal necrosis with hemorrhages spreading along the nerve fiber layer in a characteristic brushfire pattern.

title: The Great Imitator — Syphilis Unmasked
description: A 42-year-old man presents with bilateral decreased vision and vitritis. IRAF shows the pathognomonic inverse leopard spot pattern — hyperautofluorescent spots on a hypoautofluorescent background indicating outer retinal involvement.

title: Rain Clouds in the Eye — Fungal Endophthalmitis
description: A 55-year-old diabetic woman with recent ICU stay for sepsis presents with progressively decreasing vision. OCT reveals the characteristic rain-cloud sign — compact fungal balls with posterior shadowing.

title: The Fever That Came Back — Post-Infectious Vasculitis
description: A 22-year-old man presents with sudden vision loss 3 weeks after recovering from dengue fever. The delay is key — this is immune-mediated Type III vasculitis from circulating antigen-antibody complexes, not direct infection.

title: The Creeping Lesion — TB Serpiginous Choroiditis
description: A 45-year-old woman with prior TB treatment presents with recurrent vision drop. The choroidal lesion extends from the disc in a serpiginous pattern. Key question: is the vision drop from reactivation, inflammatory CNVM, or subretinal hemorrhage?

title: The Hidden Granuloma — Ocular TB
description: A 32-year-old man presents with a large choroidal elevation with a small intraretinal hemorrhage at the apex — an underrated sign indicating hypoxia and VEGF overexpression. Understanding when to add anti-VEGF to ATT + steroids is crucial.

title: Acute Post-Cataract Endophthalmitis (POD 3)
description: 68F farmer from Khammam, presents POD 3 after phaco at a peripheral camp with severe pain, hand-movements vision, hypopyon 2.5mm with fibrin, no fundal glow, and dense vitritis on B-scan. Diabetic with HbA1c 9.2. Student must work through EVS protocol decision-making, justify vitreous over AC tap, and consent the patient honestly.

title: Endogenous Klebsiella Endophthalmitis with Liver Abscess
description: 52M bus conductor from Vijayawada, diabetic, presents with OS hypopyon and dense vitritis after a week of high fever and 3 days in a peripheral nursing home. RUQ tenderness on exam. Critically, the student notices early bilateral chorioretinal foci on the OD fundus suggesting hematogenous seeding from a hepatic source. Indian endemic Klebsiella K1/K2 invasive syndrome.

title: Endophthalmitis After Anti-VEGF Injection
description: 72F retired teacher with bilateral wet AMD on monthly anti-VEGF for 18 months. Returns 2 days after her latest OS injection with progressive blurring, mild AC reaction, vitritis 2+ but no obvious hypopyon. The mild presentation belies the typical streptococcal organism profile (oropharyngeal flora). Student must navigate honest disclosure, treat appropriately, and counsel about continued treatment of the fellow eye.

title: Bleb-Related Endophthalmitis Five Years Post-Trabeculectomy
description: 65M with milky-white bleb and OD light perception 5 years after trabeculectomy with MMC. Hypopyon 2mm, dense vitritis, no Seidel leak. Streptococcus and H. influenzae predominate in BRE — much more virulent than typical post-op endophthalmitis. Student must recognise that EVS protocol does not directly apply and justify earlier, more aggressive intervention.

title: VZV Acute Retinal Necrosis with Posterior Extension
description: 32F school teacher, immunocompetent, history of childhood varicella. Presents with 5-day evolution of OD floaters, curtain, mild pain. Confluent peripheral retinal whitening 270 degrees with arteritis and posterior progression. Student must distinguish VZV from HSV ARN, justify IV over oral antiviral, and anticipate junctional PVR.

title: Tubercular Serpiginous-Like Choroiditis
description: 38F tailor from Vijayawada with month-long OS floaters, blurring, evening fever and unintended weight loss. Multifocal yellowish placoid choroidal lesions at margins of older healed scars, granulomatous KPs, and ICGA hypocyanescent spots. Mantoux 18mm and old apical scar on CXR. Student must understand presumptive diagnosis, ATT regimen, steroid sequencing, and the rifampicin-isoniazid-steroid interaction.

title: NAION vs Optic Neuritis — The Differential
description: 58M with HTN, T2DM, OSA, snorer, occasional sildenafil use. Wakes up with painless inferior altitudinal field defect OD. Disc swollen with segmental hyperaemia and 'disc at risk' fellow eye (CDR 0.1). Painless onset, nocturnal hypotension hypothesis. Student must rule out arteritic AION, send ESR/CRP, accept that no proven treatment exists, and shift focus to fellow-eye protection.

title: PDR with Vitreous Hemorrhage and Possible TRD
description: 62M auto-rickshaw driver from Visakhapatnam, T2DM 18 yrs, CKD3, partial PRP OS 2 years ago lost to follow-up. Sudden OS floaters and curtain while driving. Dense vitreous haemorrhage with B-scan suggesting tongue-like membrane and possible posterior pole traction. Student must navigate the limits of B-scan, decide on pre-op anti-VEGF timing, and acknowledge the socioeconomic context that drove the original loss-to-follow-up.

title: Central Retinal Artery Occlusion — The 4-Hour Window
description: 68M with HTN and dyslipidemia, sudden painless OD vision loss to hand movements 2 hours ago. Cherry red spot, retinal whitening, and severe RAPD. The student must rule out arteritic CRAO from GCA, activate the stroke pathway (carotid Doppler, ECG, neurology consult), consider whether they are still inside the rescue window, and counsel the patient honestly about prognosis.

title: Macula-Off Rhegmatogenous Retinal Detachment
description: 58M, high myope, presents 24 hours after lifting a heavy gas cylinder with photopsiae and a progressive curtain. OD vision 6/60, bullous superotemporal RD with macula off, single horseshoe tear at 11 o'clock. The student must explain the macula-off urgency, decide between scleral buckle, vitrectomy, or combined approach, and counsel honestly about visual recovery limits when the macula has been off.

title: Submacular Hemorrhage in Wet AMD on Aspirin
description: 70F with known wet AMD on monthly anti-VEGF, last injection 6 weeks ago. On aspirin for IHD. Sudden vision drop to HM. Fundus shows thick subretinal hemorrhage covering the macula and extending into the temporal arcades. The student must weigh pneumatic displacement (intravitreal tPA + gas + anti-VEGF) versus pars plana vitrectomy with subretinal tPA, address the aspirin question, and act inside the photoreceptor rescue window.

title: Retinopathy of Prematurity — Plus Disease, Zone II Stage 3
description: Ex-28-week preterm, BW 1100g, post-menstrual age 36 weeks. Bilateral stage 3+ ROP zone II with plus disease. The student must consent terrified parents (in plain Telugu/Hindi-influenced English), explain the rationale for anti-VEGF over laser in zone II disease, anticipate the recurrence and reactivation risk requiring weeks of follow-up, and coordinate care with the NICU team.

title: Childhood Leukocoria — Group D Retinoblastoma
description: 14-month-old infant brought by anxious mother who noticed leukocoria in flash photographs. Examination under anaesthesia confirms a large endophytic mass occupying inferior 2/3 OD with vitreous seeds, IIRC group D. Fellow eye normal, no family history. The student must communicate the diagnosis with honesty and care, explain the treatment hierarchy (life > eye > vision), and coordinate with the ocular oncology team.
